common.dateRangeError: Start date must be before end date

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.description: Overview of users, requests, and cost metrics

users.title: User Management
users.description: Manage system users, set roles and quotas. Admins have full access, regular users can only use the API.
users.name: Name
users.quotaDeltaHint: Positive to add, negative to deduct
users.passwordCopyFailed: Copy failed, please select the text manually
users.passwordSavedOnceWarning: Plaintext is not stored. Save it to a password manager before closing this dialog.
users.passwordEmptyMeansUnchanged: Leave empty to keep the password unchanged
users.targetUserNotInPage: Target user is not on this page; use search to locate

userGroups.title: User Groups
userGroups.subtitle: Manage user grouping and per-group channel/model permissions
userGroups.createHint: After creation you can configure allowed channels and models on the detail page.
userGroups.name: Name
userGroups.nameLockedTip: The default group's name cannot be changed
userGroups.statusLockedTip: The default group cannot be disabled
userGroups.modelsHint: Leave empty for unrestricted; wildcards like gpt-4* are supported
userGroups.deleteConfirmDesc: Members of this group will be moved back to the default group. Continue?
userGroups.notFound: User group not found or deleted

userPicker.moreHint: Showing first 50, type to search more

tokenPicker.emptyGlobal: No matches. Try searching by name or ID

tokens.title: Token Management
tokens.description: Manage API access tokens. Each token is bound to a user and can restrict models and expiry.
tokens.billingDescription: Track spend and token usage for each API key, then jump straight into filtered logs.
tokens.name: Name
tokens.customKeyOptionalPlaceholder: Leave empty to auto-generate, or provide your own key
tokens.weakKeyWarningDesc: This key may be easy to guess. We recommend increasing complexity. Continue anyway?
tokens.ownerChangeHint: Changing owner only affects future attribution and billing
tokens.byokOnlyTip: When enabled, this token only routes to your private channels. Requests fail if no private channel is available — no fallback to shared channels.

billing.title: Billing
billing.description: Inspect API key spend and channel cost in one place, with daily rollups and log drilldowns.
billing.adminOnly: Billing dashboards are available to administrators only.
billing.rebuildHint: Recompute daily billing rollups from raw usage logs.
billing.rebuildPickerHint: {count} jobs are running. Pick one to view progress, or start a new rebuild.
billing.rebuildJobLost: Job lost (service may have restarted); please retrigger
billing.rebuildDateRequired: At least one date is required

channelTransfer.selectPage: Select all channels on this page
channelTransfer.importDescription: Review the file and final names, then create every channel in one operation.
channelTransfer.exportDescription: Export selected channels or every result matching the current filters.
channelTransfer.plaintextImportDescription: API keys in this file will be sent to the current Master and stored using this instance's rules.
channelTransfer.plaintextExportDescription: The download contains complete API keys, headers, and proxy settings. Store it as a credential file.
channelTransfer.fileLimit: JSON, up to 16 MiB and 1,000 channels
channelTransfer.previewFailed: Could not preview the channel file
channelTransfer.selectedDescription: Export only the currently selected channels.
channelTransfer.filteredDescription: Ignore pagination and export every channel matching the current search, type, and status filters.

channels.title: Channel Management
channels.description: Configure upstream AI service channels. Supports 50+ providers with weight and priority load balancing.
channels.sectionBasicDescription: Name, protocol adapter, API key, base URL, status
channels.sectionEndpointsProtocolDescription: Enabled endpoints, protocol adapter rules, per-model overrides
channels.sectionModelsDescription: Model list, mapping, load balancing, test model
channels.organizationTip: OpenAI Organization ID for routing requests in multi-org accounts
channels.apiVersionTip: API version for Azure or Vertex AI, e.g. 2024-02-15-preview
channels.tagTip: Custom tag for channel grouping and filtering
channels.testModelTip: Model name used for connectivity testing. Falls back to /v1/models if empty
channels.autoBanTip: Automatically disable this channel when consecutive errors occur
channels.priceRatioTip: Discount or markup applied to all cost of this channel. 1.0=full price, 0.8=20% off, 0=full price (same as 1.0)
channels.freeTip: When enabled, this channel only records tokens; billing cost is always 0 and the price ratio is ignored.
channels.statusCodeMappingTip: Map upstream HTTP status codes to different values. JSON format, e.g. '{'"502": 500'}'
channels.paramOverrideTip: Override request parameters sent to upstream. JSON format, e.g. '{'"temperature": 0.7'}'
channels.headerOverrideTip: Override HTTP headers sent to upstream. JSON format, e.g. '{'"X-Custom": "value"'}'
channels.forceFormatTip: Convert upstream responses to standard OpenAI format, useful for non-standard providers
channels.thinkingToContentTip: Merge model's reasoning_content (chain-of-thought) into the content field
channels.proxyTip: Route this channel's requests through a proxy. Supports http:// and socks5://
channels.passThroughBodyTip: Skip request conversion and send the raw client request body to upstream
channels.systemPromptTip: Automatically inject this system prompt into every request
channels.systemPromptOverrideTip: When enabled, replaces the user's system message instead of prepending
channels.advancedWarning: These are provider-specific switches. Make sure you understand the impact before changing.
channels.azureResponsesVersionTip: API version for Azure OpenAI Responses API
channels.vertexKeyTypeTip: Vertex AI auth method: json (service account key) or api_key (API key)
channels.openrouterEnterpriseTip: Enable OpenRouter Enterprise response format parsing
channels.claudeBetaQueryTip: Force append ?beta=true to Anthropic API requests
channels.allowServiceTierTip: Pass service_tier parameter to upstream. Filtered by default to avoid extra charges
channels.allowInferenceGeoTip: Pass inference_geo to Claude API for data residency compliance
channels.allowSafetyIdentifierTip: Pass user identifier to upstream. Filtered by default to protect end-user privacy
channels.inlineImageUrlTip: Download image URLs and send them inline (base64) to the upstream. Use when the upstream cannot fetch remote URLs.
channels.builtinToolFallbackTip: Controls tools that the outbound protocol cannot express. Convert to function is for Responses compatibility endpoints that only accept function tools and forces the native codec.
channels.protocolOverrideTip: Force each inbound protocol to a fixed outbound protocol for this channel, overriding default negotiation. Enabled only when 2+ Endpoints are checked. Auto = default negotiation.
channels.protocolOverrideLocked: Enable ≥2 endpoints to configure protocol override mapping
channels.protocolOverrideTargetNotInEndpoints: Target protocol is not enabled in Endpoints; check the corresponding Endpoint first
channels.protocolOverridePerModelTip: Matched rule overrides the channel-level config above; model pattern is regex.
channels.thinkingPassthroughTip: For matched models, control whether IR thinking blocks are sent back to upstream as reasoning_content. Useful when an upstream model needs reasoning replayed during multi-turn tool calls. Pattern is regex; first match wins.
channels.thinkingPassthroughOnlyOpenAIChat: Only takes effect when openai_chat outbound is enabled. The current channel does not enable openai_chat — saved rules will not apply.
channels.thinkingPassthroughSendBackTip: When enabled, IR thinking blocks on assistant messages are serialized as reasoning_content. Tool-call assistant messages without thinking will get an empty placeholder. Disable for real OpenAI to avoid 4xx.
channels.disableStoreTip: Prevent store parameter from being sent to upstream. May break Codex and similar features
channels.allowIncludeObfuscationTip: Pass stream_options.include_obfuscation to upstream. Filtered by default for protection
channels.awsKeyTypeTip: Bedrock auth method: ak_sk (Access Key/Secret Key) or api_key (API key)
channels.supportedApiTypesTip: Declare which API protocols this channel supports. Empty means auto-detect from channel type. Used for intelligent protocol negotiation (passthrough when matched, fallback by priority: Responses > Chat > Claude)
channels.fieldSystemPromptInInput: Inject system prompt into user message
channels.fieldSystemPromptInInputHint: For models without system role support; off by default
channels.sectionRequestRewriteDescription: Headers, params, system prompt, proxy, status code mapping
channels.createDescription: Configure an upstream LLM channel: protocol, models, and routing roles.
channels.editDescription: Adjust the protocol, models, and routing for this channel.
channels.endpointPathTip: Relative URL path appended to Base URL. The full request URL is displayed below.
channels.passthroughEnabledTip: When enabled, same-protocol forwarding preserves all protocol-specific fields (reasoning, tool_choice, custom tool formats, etc.) for maximum compatibility
channels.useLegacyAdaptorTip: When enabled, requests are processed via the new-api compatibility layer instead of native codec
channels.manualAddPlaceholder: Enter model names, comma or Enter to add
channels.noModelForTest: No models configured for this channel
channels.agentSelectorTip: Select which agent to run this test on. Leave empty for local test.
channels.agentRouteCreateHint: Save the channel first to assign agent routes
channels.disableKeepaliveHint: Only enable for problematic upstreams; each request opens a new connection and closes it immediately
channels.resilienceHint: Leave blank to use the global default; filled fields override only this channel's resilience settings
channels.usageLimitHint: The channel is auto-disabled when it hits any limit below or the cutoff time. Periodic-window limits auto-recover after the window resets; lifetime and cutoff limits require manual re-enable.
channels.limitCutoffHint: Leave blank to disable; the channel is permanently disabled at this time (manual re-enable required).
channels.stageMetaDesc: Channel identity: name, key, base URL, tags
channels.stageRoutingDesc: How this channel is selected: status, weight, models, usage limits
channels.stageAffinityDesc: Selection phase: whether this channel joins cache stickiness, and its TTL override
channels.affinitySectionHint: Leave blank to inherit the global stickiness settings; filled fields override only this channel.
channels.affinityParticipationTip: Whether this channel joins "prefer the channel that last hit cache for the same user + model".
channels.affinityTTLOverrideTip: Overrides the global stickiness TTL; blank inherits. Range 0-86400.
channels.stageProcessingDesc: How requests are processed inside this channel (in real order)
channels.stageConnectionDesc: Provider parameters, request field policy, and network connection
channels.upstreamProviderParametersDesc: Identity and version options for the upstream API
channels.requestFieldPolicyDesc: Controls which optional request fields are sent upstream
channels.forwardClientHeadersInfo: Forwards client request headers as-is; only auth and a few managed fields are rewritten.

models.title: Model Configuration
models.description: Set input/output pricing per model (USD/1M tokens) for automatic billing.
models.autoSynced: Models are auto-extracted from channel configs. Only pricing can be edited.
models.pricingSyncDescription: Resolve recommended prices from reference sources (basellm / models.dev) and apply after comparing with current
models.reason_zero_placeholder: Likely zero placeholder — may be wrong
models.reason_cross_source_disagree: Sources differ a lot — please review

agents.title: Agent Management
agents.description: Manage distributed agent nodes. Connected agents auto-sync configuration and process API requests.

connection.confirmDescription: New Relay streams will stop until the connection is ready again.
connection.staleDenied: Refresh connection state before running an operation
connection.activeStreams: {count, plural, one {# active stream} other {# active streams}}
connection.relayUriRequired: Enter a Relay URI for Custom mode.
connection.relayUriTooLong: Relay URI must be at most 2048 bytes.
connection.relayUriInvalid: Use a valid ws:// or wss:// URI without credentials or fragments.

agents.tokenGenerated: Enrollment token generated. Copy and use when starting the agent.
agents.enrollmentStep2: 2. Run the following command on the target machine
agents.enrollmentStep3: 3. The node will auto-register and appear in the list
agents.peerRouteModeDescription: Choose whether new requests from this Agent prefer Direct or use Relay only.
agents.editLoadFailed: Could not load Agent details. Close this dialog and try again.
agents.editDescription: Edit the Agent configuration loaded from its current detail record.
agents.detailLoadFailedDescription: Check the connection to the master and try again.
agents.noResults: No connectivity data. Click Check to start.
agents.usageGuideDesc: Route requests to specific agent nodes via HTTP headers
agents.headerAgentId: Target agent by exact Agent ID
agents.headerAddressTag: Select target agent's address by tag (requires Agent-ID or Agent-Tag)
agents.cacheSubtitle: LRU cache runtime status across all agents
agents.inflightInterruptConfirmDesc: This cancels the in-flight request on that node and cannot be undone.

logs.description: View detailed API call records including token usage and costs.

playground.title: Playground
playground.description: Test conversations with AI models. Select a model and send messages to see results.

profile.title: Profile
profile.description: View your API tokens, usage stats, and remaining quota.
profile.emailTaken: This email is already in use
profile.avatarInvalidUrl: Avatar URL must start with http:// or https://

form.avatarHint: Paste an https URL; falls back to initial on error

empty.title: No routings yet
empty.desc: Create a routing to load-balance multiple models behind one name

errors.duplicateName: A routing with this name already exists in this scope
errors.cannotNestUser: User routings cannot reference other user routings

field.name: Name
field.nameHint: This is the model name clients will request
field.enabledHint: Disabled routings fall through to lower priorities
field.enabledHintUser: Disabled routings fall through to same-named global routing or real model
field.tokenHint: Filters available members; switching token preserves selections
field.tokenLockedHint: This routing belongs only to the current token and cannot be changed here.
field.noTokensTitle: At least one token is required

members.emptyHint: A routing needs at least one member

preview.title: Live preview
preview.placeholderHint: Add at least one member to see a live preview.
preview.empty: Add a member to see how traffic flows
preview.whitelistWarning: An allowlist may affect this routing
preview.whitelistAliasBlocked: This routing alias is not currently in the intersection of the token and group allowlists. Saving is allowed, but calls will be rejected.
preview.whitelistLeavesBlocked: {count} expanded real models are outside the effective allowlist and will be skipped at runtime.
preview.whitelistAllLeavesBlocked: All expanded real models are outside the effective allowlist. Saving is allowed, but this routing cannot currently produce a callable model.

rateLimiters.title: Request Rate Limiters
rateLimiters.description: Limit request concurrency or frequency by user, channel, user group, or globally; on overflow, reject directly or queue and wait.
rateLimiters.name: Name
rateLimiters.deleteConfirm: Delete this limiter? All of its bindings will be removed too. This cannot be undone.
rateLimiters.emptyHint: Create a policy to limit concurrency or request rate by dimension.
rateLimiters.createDescription: Define a policy: limit by concurrency or rate, how quota is split, and whether to reject or queue on overflow.
rateLimiters.editDescription: Adjust the dimensions and thresholds; changes take effect immediately on save.
rateLimiters.capacityHintConcurrency: Maximum number of in-flight requests at once.
rateLimiters.capacityHintRate: Maximum number of requests allowed per time window.
rateLimiters.capacityRangeError: Capacity must be at least 1; 0 would reject every request.
rateLimiters.windowMsHint: Length of the rolling window for rate counting (milliseconds).
rateLimiters.queueSizeHint: How many requests may wait once over capacity; 0 means no queue, reject immediately.
rateLimiters.queueTimeMsHint: How long a request may wait in the queue (milliseconds); 0 uses the global default.
rateLimiters.priorityHint: When multiple limiters hit the same dimension, more specific bindings win; this value fine-tunes ties.
rateLimiters.channelScopeHint: Only applies when quota is split by channel; restricts which channels this affects.
rateLimiters.sectionBindingsHint: Attach this limiter policy to specific objects. The available target types depend on the Quota Split chosen above.
rateLimiters.bindingEmpty: No objects bound yet; add one below.
rateLimiters.bindingInvalidCombo: The current quota split does not support this target type.
rateLimiters.bindingPickObject: Please select a target object first.
rateLimiters.bindingPolicyDirty: Quota Split has changed but is not saved yet. Save the policy before adjusting bindings; otherwise the available target types won't match what the backend validates against.
rateLimiters.bindingsAfterSave: After saving, you can bind this limiter to specific channels, user groups, or users here.

agentRoutes.title: Agent Routing Rules
agentRoutes.description: View and manage routing rules that direct requests from tokens or channels to specific agent nodes.
agentRoutes.targetValueRequired: Please fill in the target value

tokenTemplates.title: Token Templates
tokenTemplates.name: Name
tokenTemplates.modelsPlaceholder: Type model name and press Enter
tokenTemplates.modelsHint: Supports exact names (gpt-4o) and regex patterns (claude-.*). Leave empty to allow all models.
tokenTemplates.allowedGroupsEmptyHint: Leave empty = available to all groups

sync.title: Sync template "{name}" to tokens
sync.noChanges: All tokens already match the template; nothing to sync
sync.warning: This action is irreversible and will overwrite per-token edits

system.title: System Maintenance
system.dataCleanupDesc: Clean up expired trace and log data to free storage space
system.cleanupHourlyHint: Suggested retention for hourly buckets: 14 days (hour-grain queries only see last 7 days; +7 day buffer)
system.confirmCleanupDesc: About to delete {count} {target} records. This action cannot be undone. Continue?

agentRelay.title: Agent relay
agentRelay.description: Control the shared Relay endpoint and whether requests may fall back from Direct to Relay.
agentRelay.defaultUriDescription: Agents in Inherit mode use this URI. Leave it empty to derive from each Agent's Master URL.
agentRelay.fallbackEnabledDescription: Allow new requests to use Relay after Direct cannot be used. Turning this off stops new Relay streams while committed streams drain.
agentRelay.probeSuccessTtlDescription: Seconds to keep a successful result before checking again.
agentRelay.probeRetryMinDescription: Seconds to wait after the first failure.
agentRelay.probeRetryMaxDescription: Maximum backoff in seconds after repeated failures.
agentRelay.probeRetryMaxError: Maximum retry must be at least the minimum and no more than 3600 seconds.
agentRelay.derivedFromMasterDescription: Each Agent derives its Relay URI from its Master URL when possible; otherwise Relay remains not configured.
agentRelay.invalidUri: Use an absolute ws:// or wss:// URI without credentials, fragments, or malformed query escapes.
agentRelay.uriTooLong: Relay URI must be at most 2048 bytes.
agentRelay.loadFailed: Relay settings could not be loaded
agentRelay.refreshFailed: Relay settings could not be refreshed
agentRelay.refreshFailedDescription: Showing the last loaded values. Your unsaved changes have been kept.
agentRelay.saveFailedDescription: Check the values and try again. Your draft has been kept.

system.traceMaxBodySizeDesc: Maximum size of request/response body captured in trace data
system.proxyUrlDesc: HTTP proxy for Master to access external APIs (fetch pricing, fetch models, etc.)
system.imageInlineFetchTimeoutSecDesc: Max time to wait when fetching a remote image URL to inline as base64, range 1..300 seconds.
system.imageInlineMaxBytesDesc: Reject and fall back to the original URL if the fetched image exceeds this size (bytes), range 1024..104857600 (100 MB).
system.imageInlineConcurrencyDesc: Max number of image URLs fetched in parallel per request, range 1..32.
system.imageInlineSsrfGuardDesc: Block fetches to private, loopback, link-local, and other internal addresses before inlining a remote image.
system.imageInlineHostAllowlistDesc: Optional comma-separated host allowlist (supports *.example.com wildcards); empty means no extra host restriction beyond the SSRF guard.
system.registrationEnabledDesc: When enabled, the login page shows a sign-up entry and users can create accounts with username and password; when disabled, only admin-created accounts or SSO flows can create users.
system.oauthAutoCreate: Auto-create account on first SSO sign-in
system.oauthAutoCreateDesc: When enabled, unbound SSO identities are shown a choice page to create a new account or bind an existing one. When disabled, only binding is allowed.
system.fallbackSleepDesc: Wait time before switching to next channel on failure (ms), 0..60000
system.fallbackSleepRangeError: Fallback interval must be between 0 and 60000 ms
system.affinityEnabledDesc: Prefer the upstream channel that last hit prompt cache for the same user + model.
system.affinityTTLDesc: How long a stickiness record stays valid; refreshed on each cache activity.
system.tokenModelWhitelistSelfService: Allow users to edit token model allowlists
system.tokenModelWhitelistSelfServiceDesc: When enabled, users can edit model allowlists for their own tokens. Group allowlists remain the upper limit.
system.maxRetriesPerChannelDesc: Maximum retry attempts within a single channel (after the first attempt), range 0..10
system.maxRetriesPerChannelRangeError: Max retries per channel must be between 0 and 10
system.retryMaxChannelsDesc: Max candidate channels a single request may try (cross-channel fallback budget).
system.retryMaxChannelsRangeError: Fallback rounds must be between 1 and 100
system.retryBackoffBaseDesc: Base wait time before the first retry (ms); actual backoff grows exponentially with each attempt, range 0..60000 ms
system.retryBackoffBaseRangeError: Retry backoff base must be between 0 and 60000 ms
system.retryBackoffMaxDesc: Maximum cap on retry backoff duration (ms); prevents unbounded wait times, range 0..60000 ms
system.retryBackoffMaxRangeError: Retry backoff max must be between 0 and 60000 ms
system.breakerThresholdDesc: Number of consecutive failures before the circuit breaker opens; the channel enters a cooldown period, range 1..1000
system.breakerThresholdRangeError: Circuit breaker threshold must be between 1 and 1000
system.breakerCooldownDesc: Cooldown duration after the circuit breaker opens (ms); the channel resumes attempts after this period, range 0..3600000 ms
system.breakerCooldownRangeError: Circuit breaker cooldown must be between 0 and 3600000 ms
system.breakerEnabledDesc: When disabled, channels still retry and fail over, but failures do not open circuit breakers or skip future requests.
system.minQuotaReserve: Min quota reserve for paid channels
system.minQuotaReserveDesc: When a user's remaining quota is at or below this value, only free channels are allowed; 0 means block only at zero.
system.rateLimiterEnabledDesc: Global master switch; when off, all limiters are bypassed and requests are no longer constrained by limiters.
system.sseKeepaliveDesc: Interval for keepalive frames sent to the client while a streaming request waits in queue (ms), 1000..60000.
system.sseKeepaliveRangeError: SSE keepalive interval must be between 1000 and 60000 ms
system.queueTimeDesc: Default queue duration used when a limiter has no per-limiter queue time set (ms), 0..600000.
system.queueTimeRangeError: Default max queue time must be between 0 and 600000 ms
system.inviteEnabledDesc: When on, registration requires a valid invite code; when off, all invite features are hidden.
system.inviteMaxCodes: Max active invite codes per user
system.inviteMaxCodesDesc: Number of active (not exhausted or expired) codes a non-admin user may hold; 0 disables user code creation. Admins are unlimited.
system.inviteMaxCodesRangeError: Max codes must be an integer 0–10000
system.inviteMaxUsesDesc: Max uses a non-admin may set on a single code. Admins are unlimited.
system.inviteMaxUsesRangeError: Max uses must be an integer 1–10000
system.pricingDisagreementThresholdDesc: Compares the input + output total price from models.dev and basellm. When abs(A-B)/max(A,B) exceeds this 0..1 threshold, the recommendation is only marked for review; source priority and final price selection are unchanged.

tokenDetail.empty: This token cannot access any model right now

providers.secretChangeHint: Leave blank to keep the existing secret.

byok.name: Name
byok.baseUrlHint: Leave empty to use provider default

catalog.empty: No models available. Ask your admin to register one.

byok.sheetDescription: Bring your own provider API key. Models must be a subset of the gateway's registered model list.
byok.emptyState: No BYOK channels. Click "New" to add one.
byok.deleteDescription: This will permanently remove the BYOK channel and its API key. This action cannot be undone.

stats.description: Usage statistics from your private-channel requests. By default, BYOK requests do not deduct your gateway quota.

settings.title: BYOK Settings
settings.description: Control whether end users can bring their own provider API keys, and which base URLs are allowed.
settings.globallyEnabledDesc: When off, all BYOK creation/use is disabled across the gateway.
settings.maxChannels: Max private channels per user (global default)
settings.maxChannelsDesc: User groups can override this individually.
settings.feeRatioDesc: BYOK costs are multiplied by this ratio (e.g., 0.1 = 10% of admin-channel cost).
settings.systemRecommended: System recommended (read-only, updated with each release)
settings.deleteBaseURLChecking: Checking how many channels use this prefix…
settings.deleteBaseURLSafe: No existing channels use this prefix. Removing it is safe.
settings.deleteBaseURLWarning: {count} existing private channel(s) currently use this prefix. After removal you must still click Save to commit the change; once saved, the next time those owners try to edit their channel the BaseURL will fail validation.

groupCard.title: BYOK (Bring Your Own Key)
groupCard.enabledDesc: Defers to the global BYOK switch by default. Turn on "Use group-specific value" to control it here for this group.
groupCard.maxDesc: Defers to the global per-user limit by default. Turn on "Use group-specific value" to apply the number above.

admin.title: All BYOK Channels
admin.description: Cross-user view of all private channels. Plaintext API keys are never shown.
admin.confirmDesc: The owner will need to re-enable it themselves. Active requests using this channel will fail.

errors.quotaDisabled: BYOK channel quota is disabled by your admin
errors.privateChannelNotOwned: Not authorized for this BYOK channel

range.daysExceedHourLimit: Range exceeds 7 days for hour granularity
range.hourWindowClamped: Window clamped to last 7 days (hour granularity limit)

banner.hourlyDataStartsFrom: Hourly data starts from {date}. Use day granularity for older periods.

limiter.localSumNote: Cluster numbers are per-node local sums (v1 local enforcement)

scripts.title: Dynamic Scripts
scripts.description: Admins can write JS scripts to rewrite request bodies or reject requests
scripts.createDescription: Configure scope and write the script
scripts.name: Name
scripts.scopeHint: Channel scope only applies at the outbound (onUpstreamRequest) stage; onRequest runs before routing when no channel is chosen. For onRequest-only scripts, use model scope or global.
scripts.codeHint: Define onRequest(ctx) and/or onUpstreamRequest(ctx); mutate ctx.body or call the API below.
scripts.emptyHint: create one to rewrite request bodies or block requests
scripts.apiBodyDesc: Read & write the parsed request body
scripts.apiSetHeaderDesc: Set a header sent to the upstream
scripts.apiRemoveHeaderDesc: Remove a header sent to the upstream
scripts.apiRejectDesc: Block the request with an error
scripts.apiConsoleDesc: Write to the gateway log (with script name)

invites.title: Invite Codes
invites.description: Create and manage invite codes that control who can register
invites.unavailable: Invite code creation isn't available for your account
invites.creator: Creator
